title: Longest Consecutive Substring with K Insertions

statement.markdown: Given two strings `s` and `t`, find the length of the longest consecutive substring of `t` that can be formed by inserting not more than `k` characters from `s`. If multiple substrings have the same maximum length, return the lexicographically smallest one.
statement.inputFormat: The input will consist of two lines. The first line contains a string `s` and an integer `k` separated by a space. The second line contains a string `t`.
statement.outputFormat: Return the length of the longest consecutive substring of `t` that can be formed by inserting not more than `k` characters from `s`.
statement.notes: The characters in `s` and `t` are distinct, and the characters in `s` will not be repeated in `t`.

hints.text: First hint text, use two pointers to maintain a sliding window over string t.

editorial.title: Editorial title, longest consecutive substring with k insertions
editorial.intuition: Explanation of the correct approach, maintaining a sliding window and using two pointers to track the current and next positions.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach summary, iterating over all possible insertions and checking the length of the resulting substring.
editorial.betterSolution: Using a sliding window approach and maintaining the current and next pointers to track the longest consecutive substring.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution details, using a hashmap to store the characters in string s and a sliding window to track the longest consecutive substring.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Correctness proof, checking that the solution returns the correct length for all possible inputs.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run, explaining how the solution works for a given input.

functionSignature.cpp: bool solve(string s, int k, string t);
functionSignature.java: public boolean solve(String s, int k, String t);
functionSignature.python: def solve(self, s: str, k: int, t: str) -> bool:
